title: SA-005: Require authenticated agent credentials on all agent-scoped routes
description: Issue a server-generated agent credential at registration and require it on heartbeat, state, iteration, experiment, hypothesis, and message routes. Reject calls where the credential does not match the claimed agent_id.
notes: Reopened after host-execution analysis. Completion requires authenticated access to agent-private /api/state (including best_algorithm_code and inspiration_code), not just write-route tokens.

comments.author: m
comments.text: Reopened: Host-protection requirement: best_algorithm_code returned by agent-private /api/state is part of the local code-execution path, so write-only auth is insufficient. Reopened to require authenticated agent-private state reads as well.

title: Epic: Execute the high-ROI VRPTW performance roadmap
description: Tracks the highest-ROI solver improvements from docs/research/applying-vrptw-findings-performance-roadmap-gpt54high.md. Scope: evolve the current one-file hybrid ALNS into a mini-HGS-style engine for the 24 Homberger 400-node instances by using the full time budget, replacing weak VRPTW-hostile operators, pruning neighborhoods, introducing O(1) route-segment move evaluation, and then adding SISR and SWAP*.

title: Use nearly the full 30-second solver budget
description: Extend the internal deadline in src/vehicle_routing/algorithm/mod.rs from the current ~4.5s to a safe ~27s budget while preserving frequent save_solution() checkpoints and feasible anytime behavior under scripts/benchmark.py timeout handling.

title: Add SWAP* with candidate-pruned evaluation
description: Implement SWAP* as an endgame intensification operator, using candidate pruning and the new move-evaluation machinery so the operator is computationally viable on 400-node instances.

title: Replace intra-route 2-opt with VRPTW-native 2-opt*
description: Remove segment-reversal-centric local search as a primary operator and replace it with inter-route 2-opt* tail swapping, which preserves directionality and is a better fit for tight time-window feasibility than classical intra-route reversal.

title: Eliminate low-level ALNS bookkeeping overhead
description: Implement the cheap performance cleanups from the roadmap: replace linear membership checks in Shaw removal with a bitset or boolean map, avoid repeated rebuilds of remaining-index vectors in worst removal, and stop recomputing ALNS penalized_cost from scratch when route-distance deltas can be tracked incrementally.

title: Prune insertion and local-search neighborhoods with candidate lists
description: Reuse the existing shaw_neighbors structure as a general candidate list so greedy insertion, regret insertion, and inter-route local search only examine geographically plausible neighbors and route targets instead of scanning all route-position pairs.

title: Upgrade repair from regret-2 to regret-3
description: Strengthen the repair phase by moving from regret-2 to regret-3 insertion once candidate pruning is in place, aiming for better VRPTW repair quality without a large architectural change.

title: Introduce O(1) route-segment evaluation for relocate moves
description: Add route or segment metadata and concatenation-based evaluation helpers so relocate can be tested without cloning full routes and rescanning feasibility with is_feasible(). Include validation that the new evaluator matches current feasibility behavior.

title: Extend O(1) move evaluation to exchange, Or-opt, and 2-opt*
description: After relocate is proven out, port the rest of the hot local-search operators to the concatenation-based evaluator so exchange, Or-opt, and 2-opt* avoid route-length feasibility rescans and share the same trusted move-evaluation machinery.

title: Add SISR destroy operator to ALNS
description: Implement Slack Induction by String Removals as an additional destroy operator inside the existing adaptive-weight ALNS framework, preserving the current operator pool while adding a stronger ruin phase for clustered and slack-sensitive instances.

title: Add greedy-blink repair operator to ALNS
description: Add a greedy-blink repair operator, suitable for pairing with SISR and for diversification inside the existing ALNS framework, rather than relying only on deterministic greedy and regret-based reinsertion.
